hypothesis: Embedding filler-specific percolation-threshold constraints (enforcing at each reverse-diffusion step that the combined volume fraction of conductive fillers meets or exceeds their aspect-ratio–dependent critical volume fraction) into PCDiff will increase Coverage by at least 7 percentage points for generations conditioned on thermal conductivity ≥1.0 W/mK compared to using only non-negativity and sum-to-one constraints.
